channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: How World Bank and IMF loans are reshaping policymaking in Africa
item.description: As debt pressures grow, African governments are reassessing the trade-offs of concessional financing.

item.title: Britain’s likely PM says will work to ‘stop the suffering’ in Gaza
item.description: Andy Burnham took to social media to apologise for the Labour Party’s initial stance on Israeli attacks in Gaza.

item.title: China expands anti-sanctions toolkit, raising risks for foreign firms
item.description: Beijing is rolling out measures to expand its ability to hit back at US and EU sanctions and export controls.

item.title: Iran war live: New attacks on Iran as US says talks still on
item.description: Washington official says US not behind the latest strikes and that technical talks with Iran continue.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes toll rises to 3,889 as risk of disease grows
item.description: The United Nations launched an appeal for roughly $300 million to assist survivors of Venezuela's earthquakes

item.title: New Mexico accuses US Justice Department of impeding Epstein investigation
item.description: State Attorney General Raul Torrez accused the Trump administration of withholding records, harming his investigation.

item.title: Waiting for Moses: Africa’s sons in Russia’s war
item.description: Russia's war in Ukraine is leaving African families to count the cost.

item.title: Mbappe and Dembele net as France beat Morocco to reach World Cup semifinal
item.description: France beat Morocco 2-0 in quarterfinals at FIFA World Cup 2026 and will meet Spain or Belgium next.

item.title: Syria says captured Damascus bombing suspects are affiliated with ISIL
item.description: Two explosions in the Syrian capital earlier this week came as French President Emmanuel Macron was visiting.

item.title: How Strait of Hormuz dispute led to latest US-Iran cycle of fighting
item.description: Clashing interpretations of vague memorandum of understanding language on Hormuz at heart of escalation, analysts say.

item.title: Trump airport name change sparks backlash in Florida
item.description: Trump airport name change sparks backlash in Florida

item.title: NYT-led group asks court to sanction OpenAI in US copyright dispute
item.description: The news firms allege ChatGPT maker is hiding evidence important to what could be landmark copyright infringement trial.

item.title: US military denies involvement after explosions heard in Iran
item.description: Iranian media report loud explosions in several locations as Israeli officials say war is 'not over'.

item.title: Mexico seeks prosecutions over migrant deaths in the US
item.description: Mexico will seek state and federal prosecutions in the United States over the deaths of Mexican migrants.

item.title: UK Labour’s Burnham apologises for response to Israel’s Gaza violence
item.description: UK's likely next PM Burnham pushes for ceasefire, tougher sanctions over Gaza violence and settlement trade bans.

item.title: Canadian PM visits Saudi Arabia to strengthen energy, mining partnerships
item.description: Carney and Saudi Crown Prince Bin Salman strengthen partnerships after years of strained relations under Trudeau.

item.title: South African protesters intimidate migrants as aid group issues warning
item.description: Doctors Without Borders (MSF) warns of growing humanitarian needs as tens of thousands of African migrants displaced.

item.title: Why the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve matters amid US-Iran tensions
item.description: US oil reserves hit lowest levels since 1983 amid growing tensions with Iran and global supply concerns.

item.title: Two more men plead guilty in US for deadly 2021 human-smuggling truck crash
item.description: Five Guatemalan nationals have now plead guilty in the migrant smuggling case linked to the deaths of 55 migrants.

item.title: What happened to the beautiful game?
item.description: Has football lost its soul? From Pele’s “Joga Bonito” to billion-dollar World Cups, the game has changed.

item.title: How Lyhanna’s rape and murder put France’s justice system under scrutiny
item.description: The rape and murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna has sparked outrage in France.

item.title: Is Syria stable enough to engage with the world?
item.description: The Trump administration plans to remove Syria from list of state sponsors of 'terrorism'.

item.title: Deadly fire breaks out at shoe factory in China
item.description: A fire has torn through a shoe factory in eastern China, killing at least 28 people and trapping workers on the roof.

item.title: US expands Iran strikes Iran hitting fishing piers near nuclear plant
item.description: Iranian officials say US strikes across Bushehr province damaged civilian infrastructure

item.title: England’s Quansah banned for two matches after World Cup last-16 red card
item.description: Quansah will miss England's quarterfinal against Norway after being sent off for a sliding studs-up tackle.
